title: Strobilanthes neglecta
taxonomy: Strobilanthes neglecta is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is a member of the genus Strobilanthes, which comprises a diverse group of plants. The Acanthaceae family is a large and widespread group of flowering plants, commonly known as the acanthus family. This family is often divided into several subfamilies, including the Acanthoideae subfamily. The classification of Strobilanthes neglecta reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Acanthaceae family.

intro: Strobilanthes neglecta, also known as the 'Mountain Snow', is a species of flowering plant in the Acanthaceae family, native to the Eastern Himalayas. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Strobilanthes neglecta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being included in traditional Ayurvedic formulations, such as rasayanas and churnas.
